At current oil prices, if Iran reverts to pre-war exports (that's my assumption), Tehran would make $60-$70 billion a year in crude and other oil liquids revenue.  With the MoU waivers on offer, Iran could be able to repatriate that money. 

Pallets and pallets of oil money.

thread_tweets.text: @Baron_Lamarre Agree: https://t.co/bCTGX9SwCt
thread_tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
The oil is the money.

Even more so if Tehran can repatriate the funds.

The Qatari frozen money is nice-to-have, but at, say, $75 a barrel, Iran makes ~$175 million a DAY in oil export revenue. That's >$5 billion in just one month. That's the real money. Check for oil waivers.

tweets.text:
Schnarch’s Four Pillars of Balance in relationships

Solid flexible self — knowing who you are without needing agreement.

Quiet mind, calm heart — self-soothing (vs. needing the other to soothe you).

Grounded responding — staying regulated under their reactivity instead of fusing or fleeing. This is literally "say the true small thing and don't move."

Meaningful endurance — tolerating discomfort for growth. The friction tolerance.

thread_tweets.text:

Solid flexible self — knowing who you are without needing agreement.

Quiet mind, calm heart — self-soothing (vs. needing the other to soothe you).

Grounded responding — staying regulated under their reactivity instead of fusing or fleeing. This is literally "say the true small thing and don't move."

Meaningful endurance — tolerating discomfort for growth. The friction tolerance.
thread_tweets.full_text:
Schnarch’s Four Pillars of Balance in relationships

Solid flexible self — knowing who you are without needing agreement.

Quiet mind, calm heart — self-soothing (vs. needing the other to soothe you).

Grounded responding — staying regulated under their reactivity instead of fusing or fleeing. This is literally "say the true small thing and don't move."

Meaningful endurance — tolerating discomfort for growth. The friction tolerance.

thread_tweets.text:
Which leads to… Bowen’s differentiation of self

Low differentiation = highly reactive to others' emotional cues, loses self in relationships, can't hold boundaries under pressure — collapses into "we-ness" (everyone must feel the same or the bond feels threatened).

Two flavors of the same disease: chameleons (adjust themselves to please, fusion mode) and bullies (force others to conform, the asshole mode). Both are undifferentiated. The self-abandoner and the asshole are the same person.

The fix is the same: lower the chronic anxiety, raise the capacity to stay a separate self in contact.

Meaningful endurance — tolerating discomfort for growth. The friction tolerance.

Two flavors of the same disease: chameleons (adjust themselves to please, fusion mode) and bullies (force others to conform, the asshole mode). Both are undifferentiated. The self-abandoner and the asshole are the same person.

The fix is the same: lower the chronic anxiety, raise the capacity to stay a separate self in contact.

tweets.text:
Morning ☕️

Market Outlook for the next couple of weeks will be out shortly — obviously a lot to catch up on in the week I was afk 

FOMC today is important but I just do not see how it will materially reshape anything near-term — liquidity conditions are much looser than most think irrespective of the Fed and that’s with the curve pricing peak hawkishness under Warsh; think the highest probability outcome is a neutral stance that feels dovish in relative terms and that sends yields + dollar lower over summer 

Oil is finally down to levels where I think it looks interesting again from a R/R POV — shared in the early June Outlook that we would likely get a chance to buy sub $78 when almost every doomer has capitulated on their Hormuz hysterics and we are now at that point where the TL is full of ‘how did the experts get this so wrong?’ — I want to buy their capitulation and will be looking for long setups now I am back at my desk 

Feels good to be back 🫡
tweets.full_text:
Morning ☕️

Market Outlook for the next couple of weeks will be out shortly — obviously a lot to catch up on in the week I was afk 

FOMC today is important but I just do not see how it will materially reshape anything near-term — liquidity conditions are much looser than most think irrespective of the Fed and that’s with the curve pricing peak hawkishness under Warsh; think the highest probability outcome is a neutral stance that feels dovish in relative terms and that sends yields + dollar lower over summer 

Oil is finally down to levels where I think it looks interesting again from a R/R POV — shared in the early June Outlook that we would likely get a chance to buy sub $78 when almost every doomer has capitulated on their Hormuz hysterics and we are now at that point where the TL is full of ‘how did the experts get this so wrong?’ — I want to buy their capitulation and will be looking for long setups now I am back at my desk 

Feels good to be back 🫡

Bernstein is legit the dumbest analyst firm I’ve seen calling for a 50% crash in Kioxia.

They gave $INTC a $36 PT back in Jan and now it’s $118.

Good lesson to ignore institutional reports that get published for retail consumption. 

They’re not here to help retail investors.

tweets.text:
I’m not sure why many folks are super bearish on my high conviction $AAOI long… 

Ever since $30, then on the way up to $170. (Yes I do think every bear is wrong, we’ll see who’s right).

They have scarce laser capacity that $AMD and other hyperscalers are looking for.

While the entire industry is bottlenecked by $NVDA. 

Along with a US transceiver supply chain for mass production of 800g/1.6T (management - largest in America).

While demand far exceeds supply and while assembly gets outsourced to Asia.

Then they’re quoting $471M monthly revenue in H1 entering H2 of 2027. Which is $5.6B ARR, off a $13.5B MC…

While a lot of major inflection volume hits even later in 2028.

As for fluctuations, there might be active $600M ATMs that get tapped into at random times. 

And random bear posts + macro from time to time that cause more volatility (eg. Analyst notes saying bear on $LITE due to false CPO delay rumors, then that brings down others in the sector).

Also we’re a year out so timelines are still a little early.

I haven’t seen such fast revenue ramp since $NBIS.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: @aleabitoreddit AAOI又高跳下落，对此你怎么看

tweets.text:
HIP-3 daily volume has now reached it's highest 7D MA reaching 41% of total volume executed on Hyperliquid.

HIP-3 will be Hyperliquids most dominant product in terms of volume executed and will exceed 50% of all volume executed.

Perpetuals on traditional markets offer a much larger TAM than perpetuals on digital assets as it currently stands.

It will be the single largest driver of USDC to Hyperliquid.

tweets.text:
We're thinking of switching from annualized numbers for revenue to TTM (last trailing 12 months)

what should we do for projects that are younger than 12mo?
- use ttm anyway
- annualize based on the data we have (eg if live for 6mo, multiply by 2)
- dont use ttm, use 30d instead https://t.co/9Bky8fkjUz

thread_tweets.text:
@euler_mab The goal with including these is to show investors the ceiling of how much the protocol could charge, eg if it aggressively increased its take rate it could grow rev up until that number

Besides that, its useful to see which services ppl actually pay for
thread_tweets.full_text:
@euler_mab The goal with including these is to show investors the ceiling of how much the protocol could charge, eg if it aggressively increased its take rate it could grow rev up until that number

tweets.full_text:
We're thinking of switching from annualized numbers for revenue to TTM (last trailing 12 months)

what should we do for projects that are younger than 12mo?
- use ttm anyway
- annualize based on the data we have (eg if live for 6mo, multiply by 2)
- dont use ttm, use 30d instead https://t.co/9Bky8fkjUz

@KyleReidhead we could also just do 30d alone and only display the revenue in the last 30d, would that be better?

@euler_mab The goal with including these is to show investors the ceiling of how much the protocol could charge, eg if it aggressively increased its take rate it could grow rev up until that number

Besides that, its useful to see which services ppl actually pay for

tweets.text:
Mizuho Research: No delays on CPO or 800v dc. 

Revised up optical engine projections from $NVDA demand ramp.  

- Next phase in CPO for long term, believes InP DFB lasers remains the focus (hello $SIVE). VSCEL and microLED remain "unproven" in short distance in rack + chip to chip for 1.6T+ 

- HVDC deployment on track, 800VDC incremental volume 2027, with higher penetration in 2028.

What a stupid CPO related selloff recently.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
$NVDA Networking Senior Vice President refuting recent analyst reports on delays:

- “ the most exciting stuff is co-packaged optics.”
- There is no delay in H2 CPO product delivery schedule. 
- CPO switch will enter mass production and begin ramping up customer deliveries as https://t.co/OtFUvMgqRY
tweets.full_text:
Mizuho Research: No delays on CPO or 800v dc. 

Revised up optical engine projections from $NVDA demand ramp.  

- Next phase in CPO for long term, believes InP DFB lasers remains the focus (hello $SIVE). VSCEL and microLED remain "unproven" in short distance in rack + chip to chip for 1.6T+ 

- HVDC deployment on track, 800VDC incremental volume 2027, with higher penetration in 2028.

What a stupid CPO related selloff recently.

I genuinely don’t understand this:

1. Google AI Mode has access to tons and tons and tons and tons and tons  and TONS of sites that ChstGPT and Claude can’t access
2. If you’re in Chrome it is one million times easier to use the search bar for a query
3. It is objectively better for all local and navigation searches 

Not using Google anymore is literally just using the internet in a worse way. Not even picking on Signull I’ve seen plenty of “tech forward” people say this. It makes no sense to me.

tweets.text:
what an unexpectedly insane day for chinese AI

- deepseek raises at $50B val

- then microsoft (openai’s biggest investor) announces it’s using deepseeks model for its flagship copilot ai

- then Zhipu drops glm-5.2 which matches opus 4.8 and got 5.5 at top coding benchmarks

all open weight models btw. if you’re an american enterprise spending $10-100M+ a year on claude code/codex you have to at least be *thinking* about using these models

imagine wrapping these models in a cursor harness, performance would be very competitive. 

very significant in light of recent government bans on fable too
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Introducing GLM-5.2: Frontier Intelligence, Open Weights

- Significant improvements in coding and agentic tasks
- Strong long-horizon capabilities with a 1M context window
- Two levels of reasoning effort: GLM-5.2 (max) pushes the limits, while GLM-5.2 (high) strikes a strong https://t.co/SjGPSVhePJ
tweets.full_text:
what an unexpectedly insane day for chinese AI

- deepseek raises at $50B val

- then microsoft (openai’s biggest investor) announces it’s using deepseeks model for its flagship copilot ai

- then Zhipu drops glm-5.2 which matches opus 4.8 and got 5.5 at top coding benchmarks

all open weight models btw. if you’re an american enterprise spending $10-100M+ a year on claude code/codex you have to at least be *thinking* about using these models

imagine wrapping these models in a cursor harness, performance would be very competitive. 

very significant in light of recent government bans on fable too

tweets.text: @buccocapital the difference is behavioural. chatgpt / claude  literally FEELS more like your “homepage” than google does. it also has memory and context about you  which means it’s more likely to “get” what your asking for immediately.
